text: The surgeon uses a barb suture (V-lock) to secure the cystic duct stump in a subtotal cholecystectomy. An anchoring stitch is placed at the near end, followed by snug running sutures along the stump. The surgeon takes small interval bites approximately two millimeters apart, ensuring tight closure to prevent bile leakage. The neck and cystic duct junction have been divided, allowing tissue retraction. The surgeon completes the suturing by taking an interlock stitch and divides the remaining tissue using scissors.
